analysis.btc_price_note: BTC ~$77,900 as of Apr 27 ~17:30 UTC (range $76,800-$79,032 today). Hit wall at $80,000 — concentrated sell orders capped gains. Support $77,500 / $75,900.
sentiment_key_signals: Fear & Greed Index: 47 (Neutral) — improved from 33 (Fear) yesterday and 29 last week
Crypto market cap $2.71T, +1.7% led by BTC and ETH
BTC dominance 58.2% — strong relative strength vs altcoins
$80,000 psychological resistance NOT broken — sell wall capped rally
$2.44B spot BTC ETF inflows this month (best since Oct 2025 all-time highs)
Short squeeze supports price but trend remains unconfirmed per Investing.com
FOMC Apr 28-29: rate hold near-certain but hawkish Powell tone is key risk
Iran peace proposal sent to Washington; Hormuz reopening still uncertain
No CPI/PPI releases in next 24h (CPI next release May 12, PPI May 13)
analysis.macro_backdrop: FOMC Day 1 starts Apr 28; rate decision Apr 29 18:00 UTC. Rate hold ~100% priced. Biggest risk = hawkish Powell tone. ETF inflows positive but $80K resistance unbroken. F&G recovered to Neutral (47) suggesting stabilizing sentiment. No CPI/PPI in 24h window. Geopolitical: Iran-Hormuz remains uncertain variable.
analysis.regime_rationale: Neutral (0.45): Market shows improving sentiment (F&G 47 vs 31 yesterday) and strong ETF inflows, but $80K resistance is a clear ceiling. FOMC beginning tomorrow creates pre-event uncertainty. Confidence below 0.50 — bot macro filter INACTIVE for this cycle. Bullish signals: ETF inflows record pace, F&G recovering, institutional buying. Bearish/caution signals: $80K wall, unconfirmed trend, FOMC overhang, Iran geopolitical risk.
